about.pageSubtitle: Aspiring AI Engineer · BCA Student · Based in Kathmandu, Nepal
about.educationQuote: Constant learning is the only way to navigate the evolving digital landscape.
bio: I am Sawan Mukhiya, an aspiring AI/ML Engineer, Fullstack Developer, and Web Scraper from Kathmandu. I love the thrill of turning ideas into reality, whether that means training a neural network to recognize text, designing a clean REST API, or scraping hard-to-reach data from the web. I enjoy working at the intersection of intelligence and software — where messy data transforms into clean, impactful applications.

Currently pursuing my Bachelor in Computer Application at National College of Computer Studies, my days are a mix of studying and building. I spend my time training machine learning models using PyTorch and Scikit-learn, developing full-stack web apps with Django and React, and engineering robust web scrapers with Playwright.

Whether I'm building my OCR platform SnipTxt, predicting productivity with XGBoost, or structuring complex web data into clean JSON schemas, my philosophy is simple: write code that solves real problems and makes life a little easier for everyone.
about.philosophyQuote: Building intelligent systems that make data speak — from raw numbers to real decisions.
about.philosophyBody: I believe the best AI isn't the most complex — it's the most useful. Every project I build is a step toward software that genuinely helps people understand and act on information around them.

projects.title: SnipTxt: OCR Platform
projects.category: AI / Machine Learning · Full Stack Web
projects.description: A web OCR system built with CNN + BiLSTM + CTC for text recognition. Features modular REST APIs and a Django backend integrated with a MySQL database for storing extracted text.
longDescription: SnipTxt is a full-stack Optical Character Recognition (OCR) platform that accepts an image and outputs recognized text with high accuracy. The system is powered by a custom deep learning model that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) layers, and a Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) decoder — a state-of-the-art architecture for sequence-to-sequence text recognition tasks.

The backend is built on Django REST Framework, exposing modular APIs that handle image uploads, model inference, and text storage. A React.js frontend provides an intuitive interface for users to upload images and receive extracted text instantly. All recognized text is persisted to a MySQL database, enabling users to maintain a history of their OCR queries.

The model was trained using supervised learning on labeled text-image datasets, learning to map visual patterns of characters directly to their Unicode representations without requiring explicit character segmentation.
highlights: Built CNN + BiLSTM + CTC deep learning model for end-to-end text recognition
Designed modular Django REST APIs for image upload and inference
Integrated React.js frontend with real-time OCR result display
Persisted results to MySQL with searchable extraction history
Implemented backend testing and robust error handling for production stability
Achieved high character recognition accuracy on diverse text image inputs

projects.title: NEPSE Stock Journal
projects.description: A stock journal with tax/fee calculations and portfolio analytics for the Nepal Stock Exchange (NEPSE). Backend APIs handle financial reporting and data aggregation for investor insights.
longDescription: The NEPSE Stock Journal is a data-driven web application designed specifically for Nepal Stock Exchange (NEPSE) investors. It allows users to record their stock trades and automatically computes the true cost of each transaction by factoring in all applicable taxes, broker fees, SEBON fees, and DP charges — giving a precise profit/loss figure that manual spreadsheets often miss.

The application is built with a Django REST Framework backend that handles all business logic for trade journalization, tax calculations, and portfolio analytics. A React.js frontend presents interactive charts and tables, enabling users to visualize their portfolio performance over time, drill into individual stock positions, and generate financial insights.

All trade data is stored in a MySQL database, with carefully designed schemas to support both individual trade queries and aggregated portfolio reporting. The system also supports saving and retrieving historical trade data, making it a complete investment tracking solution for the Nepali market.
highlights: Implemented NEPSE-specific tax/fee calculation engine (broker, SEBON, DP charges)
Built RESTful APIs for trade journalization and portfolio analytics
Developed interactive chart visualizations for portfolio performance tracking
Designed MySQL schemas for both granular trade data and aggregated reporting
Enabled profit/loss analysis across individual stocks and entire portfolio
Implemented data validation and testing scripts to ensure financial accuracy

projects.title: ExEC: Expression Tool
projects.description: A web app for evaluating and converting mathematical expressions. Uses MySQL to store and query expression data and conversion results, with a clean PHP backend.
longDescription: ExEC (Expression Evaluation & Conversion Tool) is a web application that provides a comprehensive environment for working with mathematical expressions in multiple notations. Users can input expressions in infix, prefix, or postfix notation, and the system evaluates them and converts between notations seamlessly.

The application is built with a PHP backend that handles the parsing, evaluation, and conversion logic using stack-based algorithms — the same algorithms studied in Data Structures & Algorithms coursework. A MySQL database stores expression inputs and their evaluated results, enabling users to maintain an expression history and look up previous calculations.

The frontend provides a clean, intuitive interface where users can select their input notation, enter an expression, and instantly see evaluation results alongside notation conversions. The tool is particularly useful for computer science students learning about expression trees and notation systems.
highlights: Implemented infix-to-prefix, infix-to-postfix, and postfix-to-infix conversion algorithms
Built expression evaluator using stack-based algorithms
Stored expression history and results in MySQL for query and retrieval
Designed clean PHP backend with modular logic for parsing and evaluation
Useful academic tool for understanding algorithmic expression handling
Supports multiple mathematical operators with correct precedence rules

projects.title: Chess Arena
projects.description: An offline chess game built in Java with full undo, save/load, and draw features. MySQL is used to persist game states, moves, and user sessions across play sessions.
longDescription: Chess Arena is a fully functional offline chess game implemented in Java, designed with a clean object-oriented architecture. The game supports all standard chess rules including castling, en passant, and pawn promotion, along with quality-of-life features that make it a complete chess experience.

The application uses MySQL as a persistent storage layer, enabling players to save their ongoing games and resume them at any time. The game state — including the board position, move history, and player information — is serialized and stored in structured database tables. The move history can be queried to replay games or analyze past decisions.

Key gameplay features include an undo system that allows players to revert any number of moves, a draw offer mechanism between two players, and the ability to save/load named game sessions. The game is built using Java Swing for the graphical interface, with a clear separation between the game logic (model), UI (view), and controller layers.
highlights: Implemented full chess rule set: castling, en passant, pawn promotion, check/checkmate
Built undo system allowing move history traversal
Designed save/load system with MySQL-backed game state persistence
Used Java Swing for the interactive graphical chessboard interface
Stored move history, board states, and user sessions in relational database
Applied MVC architecture for clean separation of game logic and UI

projects.title: Web-Scraping Pipeline
projects.description: Automated scraper using Playwright to extract daily entertainment articles and media. Features robust error handling, automated navigation, and extraction into structured JSON.
longDescription: The Web-Scraping Pipeline is a production-grade automated data extraction system built in Python using Playwright for browser automation. The primary target is ekantipur.com, one of Nepal's leading news portals, from which the scraper extracts the top 5 daily entertainment articles and the cartoon of the day.

The scraper uses the UV package manager for dependency isolation and reproducible environments. Playwright handles headless browser automation, navigating JavaScript-heavy pages that traditional HTTP-based scrapers cannot handle. The system implements robust error handling and validation checks at each extraction step to ensure data accuracy even when the target website's structure changes.

Extracted data — including article titles, URLs, summaries, images, and publication timestamps — is parsed, normalized, and saved into structured JSON files. The pipeline is designed to be run on a schedule, making it suitable for building datasets, monitoring content, or feeding downstream data applications.
highlights: Used Playwright for JavaScript-heavy page scraping with headless browser automation
Extracted top 5 entertainment articles + daily cartoon from ekantipur.com
Implemented multi-layer error handling and validation for scraping resilience
Engineered automated navigation logic for dynamic page structures
Normalized extracted data into structured JSON schemas for downstream use
Used UV for dependency management and reproducible environments

projects.title: Productivity Prediction
projects.description: Garment employee productivity prediction model. Performed extensive EDA and built regression models using XGBoost, achieving high predictive accuracy (R² ≈ 0.94).
longDescription: This data science project focuses on predicting the productivity of garment factory employees using machine learning regression techniques. The dataset contains features such as team size, targeted productivity, overtime, incentive pay, idle time, and department — all factors that influence worker output in a manufacturing environment.

The project begins with an extensive Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) phase, using Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn to visualize productivity patterns, understand feature distributions, identify correlations, and detect outliers. Insights from EDA directly informed the feature engineering decisions.

Multiple regression models were trained and evaluated using Scikit-learn, including Linear Regression, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosted Trees. The final XGBoost model achieved an R² score of approximately 0.94, meaning the model explains 94% of the variance in employee productivity — a highly accurate result for this domain.
highlights: Performed comprehensive EDA to understand productivity patterns and feature relationships
Applied feature engineering based on domain knowledge of garment manufacturing
Trained and compared multiple regression models: Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost
Achieved R² ≈ 0.94 with final XGBoost model — explaining 94% of productivity variance
Used Seaborn and Matplotlib for rich visualization of data distributions and model results
Delivered a complete end-to-end data science workflow: EDA → preprocessing → modeling → evaluation

projects.title: Hands On Machine Learning
projects.description: Implementation of various machine learning algorithms and concepts using Python and Scikit-learn, emphasizing supervised learning, evaluation metrics, and practical applications.
longDescription: Hands On Machine Learning is a comprehensive study project that systematically implements and explores core machine learning algorithms using Python's scientific stack. Inspired by hands-on learning philosophy, the project serves as both a study companion and a practical reference for ML concepts.

The project covers a wide spectrum of supervised learning algorithms — including Linear Regression, Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Random Forests, Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Neural Network basics. Each algorithm is implemented, trained, and evaluated on relevant datasets with thorough documentation of observations.

Key concepts explored include model evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC), cross-validation strategies, hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV, feature scaling and preprocessing pipelines, and overfitting/underfitting diagnostics. All implementations use Scikit-learn with NumPy and Pandas for data handling, and Matplotlib/Seaborn for visualizations.
highlights: Implemented 7+ supervised learning algorithms from scratch and with Scikit-learn
Explored model evaluation metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC
Applied cross-validation and GridSearchCV for robust model selection
Built feature preprocessing pipelines for handling missing values and scaling
Investigated ensemble methods: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost
Documented observations and findings in Jupyter Notebooks for reproducibility
